north_star_guard: Projections, liveness, status snapshots, and support labels MUST NEVER serve as positive action authority. Any reader surface classified as projection, liveness, status_snapshot, or support_label is explicitly marked is_authority=false in this registry.

rows.current_contract: Cloud/status/watchdog reader surfaces (liveness, status snapshots, watchdog)
rows.target_contract: M7 cloud reader adapter — reads are informational only, never increase authority
rows.boundary_conditions: Cloud reads must be informational; cannot be used to skip/unblock/advance work
rows.fail_closed: Read is denied; cloud service returns UNKNOWN; incident logged
rows.proof: Cloud reader enrollment + informational disposition enforced
rows.rollback_policy: Disable cloud reader; no direct status snapshot fallback for authority decisions
rows.mixed_version_policy: Cloud service version pinned; version bumps require adapter re-enrollment
rows.retirement_gate: After all cloud reader callers migrate to enrolled adapters
rows.non_authority_reason: liveness cannot be positive authority per North Star; projections, liveness, status snapshots, and support labels must not skip/unblock/advance work

rows.fail_closed: Read is denied; cloud service returns UNKNOWN; incident logged
rows.proof: Cloud reader enrollment + informational disposition enforced
rows.rollback_policy: Disable cloud reader; no direct status snapshot fallback for authority decisions
rows.mixed_version_policy: Cloud service version pinned; version bumps require adapter re-enrollment
rows.retirement_gate: After all cloud reader callers migrate to enrolled adapters
rows.non_authority_reason: status_snapshot cannot be positive authority per North Star; projections, liveness, status snapshots, and support labels must not skip/unblock/advance work

rows.current_contract: Compatibility shim/adapter bridging old and new reader surfaces
rows.target_contract: M7 compatibility reader enrollment with version gating and expiry
rows.boundary_conditions: Shim must prove both old and new reader identities; cannot introduce authority
rows.fail_closed: Shim returns error; both paths blocked; incident logged
rows.proof: Compatibility adapter enrollment + version gate test + zero-authority proof
rows.rollback_policy: Disable shim; old readers see UNKNOWN; no dual read authority
rows.mixed_version_policy: Shim explicitly versioned with expiry
rows.retirement_gate: After all old readers are migrated and shim expiry date has passed

rows.current_contract: Compatibility shim/adapter bridging old and new reader surfaces
rows.target_contract: M7 compatibility reader enrollment with version gating and expiry
rows.boundary_conditions: Shim must prove both old and new reader identities; cannot introduce authority
rows.fail_closed: Shim returns error; both paths blocked; incident logged
rows.proof: Compatibility adapter enrollment + version gate test + zero-authority proof
rows.rollback_policy: Disable shim; old readers see UNKNOWN; no dual read authority
rows.mixed_version_policy: Shim explicitly versioned with expiry
rows.retirement_gate: After all old readers are migrated and shim expiry date has passed

rows.current_contract: Historical reader path (WBC evidence, audit trail, milestone artifacts)
rows.target_contract: M7 historical reader enrollment — reads are rebuildable from repo evidence
rows.boundary_conditions: Historical reads must be content-addressed and rebuildable from repo evidence
rows.fail_closed: Historical read is denied if evidence is missing; UNKNOWN is returned
rows.proof: WBC evidence path + content-addressed hash verification
rows.rollback_policy: Disable historical reader; evidence remains available via git history
rows.mixed_version_policy: Historical evidence is commit-bound; version is the commit SHA
rows.retirement_gate: After all consumers migrate to direct evidence access
rows.non_authority_reason: Historical evidence is content-addressed and rebuildable. It provides audit trail and verification but does not mint new completion authority.

rows.current_contract: Advisory/execution projection — explicitly NOT authority
rows.target_contract: M7 projection gate: projections are rebuildable diagnostics, never authority
rows.boundary_conditions: Projection MUST NOT be used to skip/unblock/advance work
rows.fail_closed: Projection returns advisory diagnostics only; authority decision is always UNKNOWN
rows.proof: Non-authority classification in inventory + zero-authority acceptance proof
rows.rollback_policy: Disable projection without restoring legacy reads
rows.mixed_version_policy: Projection version is pinned to commit
rows.retirement_gate: After all upstream consumers stop relying on projection for authority
rows.non_authority_reason: Projections are rebuildable diagnostics, never authority. Accepting a projection as authority would violate the North Star (projections cannot skip/unblock/advance work).

rows.current_contract: Advisory/execution projection — explicitly NOT authority
rows.target_contract: M7 projection gate: projections are rebuildable diagnostics, never authority
rows.boundary_conditions: Projection MUST NOT be used to skip/unblock/advance work
rows.fail_closed: Projection returns advisory diagnostics only; authority decision is always UNKNOWN
rows.proof: Non-authority classification in inventory + zero-authority acceptance proof
rows.rollback_policy: Disable projection without restoring legacy reads
rows.mixed_version_policy: Projection version is pinned to commit
rows.retirement_gate: After all upstream consumers stop relying on projection for authority
rows.non_authority_reason: AcceptedAttemptProjection is a read-only execute projection built from accepted dispatch envelopes. It carries diagnostic data but must never mint completion authority by itself.

rows.current_contract: Provider/dynamic reader surface (plugin, generated code)
rows.target_contract: M7 provider reader enrollment with static manifest
rows.boundary_conditions: Provider must be enrolled; generated code must be verifiable
rows.fail_closed: Provider read is blocked; fallback to UNKNOWN; incident logged
rows.proof: Provider enrollment manifest + static verification + read receipt
rows.rollback_policy: Disable provider via enrollment revocation
rows.mixed_version_policy: Provider version pinned; generated code content-addressed
rows.retirement_gate: After all consumers migrate to enrolled provider
rows.non_authority_reason: Provider readers are dynamic surfaces that cannot be statically verified. They must not serve as authority until enrolled in M7.

rows.current_contract: Authority reader route defined in authority_readers.py or WBC boundary inventory
rows.target_contract: M7 controlled-authoritative-reader gate with read-only fence
rows.boundary_conditions: Reader must receive a read-only token; cannot mutate state or advance work
rows.fail_closed: Reject read attempt; log observability; do not unlock dependents
rows.proof: M6 inventory row + enrolled in authority_readers.py + disposition audit
rows.rollback_policy: Disable reader promotion without restoring legacy reads; keep audit trail
rows.mixed_version_policy: Old consumers see stale terminal labels until authority adapter is enrolled
rows.retirement_gate: After all callers migrate to enrolled reader and legacy path has zero consumers

rows.rollback_policy: Disable reader path via feature flag; no legacy read fallback
rows.mixed_version_policy: Resident modules version-locked to repo commit
rows.retirement_gate: After all callers migrate to WBC-mediated read path

rows.current_contract: Shell/wrapper script that reads authority-facing state
rows.target_contract: M7 wrapper reader: shell scripts must route through authority-reader adapter
rows.boundary_conditions: Must produce content-addressed read receipt; raw status reads are denied
rows.fail_closed: Script execution fails; read is denied; observability event logged
rows.proof: Wrapper enrollment in M7 reader registry + content-addressed read receipt
rows.rollback_policy: Remove execute permission or replace with read-only stub; never restore legacy read authority
rows.mixed_version_policy: Wrapper version pinned to repo commit; old versions rejected
rows.retirement_gate: After replacement Python reader is landed and all callers are migrated

row_hash_coverage: each row hash computed from deterministically ordered JSON excluding row_hash field
fail_closed_default: Any reader whose authority classification is uncertain defaults to fail-closed (deny). Readers must be explicitly enrolled to serve authority reads.
